title: Pedilonum platycaulon
taxonomy: Pedilonum platycaulon is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, Pedilonum platycaulon is classified within the larger group of plants known as angiosperms. More specifically, it falls under the class of Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants.

The plant's leaves and roots are used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Pedilonum platycaulon, also known as the Pedilanthus plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Pedilanthus plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pedilanthus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tincture or ointment to treat various ailments.
